pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Default palette for solo works without an explicit anchor
series_anchor.palette: raw umber, sepia, parchment cream, single restrained accent appropriate to the work
series_anchor.mood: Quiet, unfussy, intent. The atmosphere of a single book on a single table.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: One dominant warm wash, one supporting tone, sparing accent. Generous paper exposure.

1_context.content_excerpt:
## CHAP. 1: CHAPTER ONE.

> Of the various ways of treating the moral and legislative sciences; of the phenomena necessary to know in order to master these sciences; of the causes that have arrested or favored their progress; and of the object of this work.

In writing on legislation, my goal is not to present a system of laws, nor to attack or defend the institutions of any particular country; I wish simply to investigate the causes that make a people prosper or decline, or that render it stationary. To devote myself to these investigations, I need neither to imagine systems nor to reason fro

output.reasoning: Comte's treatise investigates the conditions that cause societies to prosper, decline, or stagnate — a project of careful observation and systematic inquiry. A single brass balance scale, slightly tilted and unadorned, evokes the weighing of causes and conditions without illustrating any specific law or institution. It carries the Enlightenment's empirical spirit: measured, rational, quietly serious.
output.subject: A single brass balance scale, one pan slightly lower than the other, resting on a bare surface
output.composition: The scale is placed left of center, occupying roughly the lower third of the composition, with the right side and upper two-thirds left as open warm cream paper. The slight tilt of the pans creates asymmetric visual tension that anchors the eye without centering the subject.
output.watercolor_treatment: A wet-on-wet wash of raw umber lays the dominant tone, pooling darker where the scale's arm and hanging chains cast implied shadow, diluting to near-nothing at the edges. Yellow ochre is introduced wet into the brass surfaces while the umber is still damp, bleeding softly into it. Sepia is applied in a second dry-brush pass along the underside of the beam and the heavier pan, leaving visible paper grain in the lighter areas. Pencil under-drawing is faintly legible at the chain links.
output.prompt: A single brass balance scale rests left of center on warm cream cold-press cotton paper, one pan hanging fractionally lower than the other. A wet-on-wet wash of raw umber establishes the dominant tone, pooling darkest beneath the central beam and along the hanging chains, then diluting to almost nothing toward the edges of the composition. Yellow ochre bleeds into the brass surfaces while the umber is still damp, the two pigments merging softly without a hard boundary. A second pass of sepia, applied with a nearly dry brush, scuffs along the underside of the beam and the heavier pan, leaving cold-press paper grain fully visible in the thinner passages. Faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible at the chain links. The upper right two-thirds of the painting is open, unpainted paper — deliberate, breathing, restrained.

output.reasoning: Only material issue is a weak-language hedge in the dilution description; gate logic still permits shipping with one medium issue.

issues.problematic_phrase: "then diluting to almost nothing toward the edges of the composition."
issues.suggestion: "then diluting to a transparent edge at the margins of the composition."

output.reasoning: The critic identifies one medium-severity issue: the phrase 'then diluting to almost nothing toward the edges of the composition' is weak and vague. The suggested replacement 'then diluting to a transparent edge at the margins of the composition' is more precise and declarative without bloating the prompt or conflicting with the series anchor. This is a clean, surgical improvement worth accepting.

decisions.reason: The replacement is more precise and declarative, removing hedging language while preserving the intended visual effect of generous paper exposure.

output.final_prompt: A single brass balance scale rests left of center on warm cream cold-press cotton paper, one pan hanging fractionally lower than the other. A wet-on-wet wash of raw umber establishes the dominant tone, pooling darkest beneath the central beam and along the hanging chains, then diluting to a transparent edge at the margins of the composition. Yellow ochre bleeds into the brass surfaces while the umber is still damp, the two pigments merging softly without a hard boundary. A second pass of sepia, applied with a nearly dry brush, scuffs along the underside of the beam and the heavier pan, leaving cold-press paper grain fully visible in the thinner passages. Faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible at the chain links. The upper right two-thirds of the painting is open, unpainted paper — deliberate, breathing, restrained.
output.changes_summary: One phrase was revised: 'diluting to almost nothing toward the edges' was replaced with 'diluting to a transparent edge at the margins' to remove hedging and sharpen the description of the wash's fade.

final_prompt: A single brass balance scale rests left of center on warm cream cold-press cotton paper, one pan hanging fractionally lower than the other. A wet-on-wet wash of raw umber establishes the dominant tone, pooling darkest beneath the central beam and along the hanging chains, then diluting to a transparent edge at the margins of the composition. Yellow ochre bleeds into the brass surfaces while the umber is still damp, the two pigments merging softly without a hard boundary. A second pass of sepia, applied with a nearly dry brush, scuffs along the underside of the beam and the heavier pan, leaving cold-press paper grain fully visible in the thinner passages. Faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible at the chain links. The upper right two-thirds of the painting is open, unpainted paper — deliberate, breathing, restrained.
